title: InputBibliography
description: A collection of bibliographic references with optional metadata.

sets.description:
Optional compound entry sets keyed by set id.

Each set id maps to an ordered list of reference ids that should be treated
as one compound numeric group when `compound-numeric` is enabled by style.

ArchiveInfo.description:
Structured archival location metadata for unpublished and archival material.

Models the hierarchical location of an item within an archive, following
common archival description standards (DACS, ISAD(G), EAD).

box.description:
Box number within the collection.

Uses raw identifier syntax `r#box`, which serde serializes as `"box"` transparently.

collection.description: Name of the collection within the archive.

collection-id.description: Identifier for the collection (call number, accession number, etc.).

location.description:
Display override for the archival location (shelfmark, call number, or complex location string).

When present, overrides the structured fields for display. Acts as the legacy
`archive_location` fallback for complex shelfmarks that don't fit the structured model.

name.description:
Name of the archive or repository holding the material.

Uses `MultilingualString` to support international institution names
(e.g., 国立国会図書館 / National Diet Library).

place.description:
Geographic location (city/country) of the archive.

Parallel to `SimpleName.location`; both carry geographic-place semantics.

series.description: Name of the series within the collection.

url.description: URL for the archival item (e.g. digitized finding aid or item page).

Classic.description: A classic work (Aristotle, Bible, etc.) with standard citation forms.

issued.description: Publication date of this edition (not original)

section.description: Section, book, or chapter in standard reference system

Collection.description: A collection of works, such as an anthology or proceedings.

short-title.description: Optional short form of the parent title for style-directed rendering.

CollectionComponent.description: A component of a larger monograph, such as a chapter in a book.

CollectionType.description: Discriminates collection subtypes for style-directed formatting.

oneOf.description: A curated anthology of independent works (e.g., short stories, essays).
Published proceedings of a conference or symposium.
A book assembled from contributions by multiple authors under an editor.
An edited volume that may span multiple books or a series.

Contributor.description: A contributor can be a single string, a structured name, or a list of contributors.

format.description:
File format. Prefer IANA media types (e.g., `"text/csv"`) or common
abbreviations (e.g., `"NetCDF"`, `"HDF5"`) where no IANA type exists.

EprintInfo.description:
Preprint server identifier following the biblatex eprint model.

Used on all three reference classes (Monograph, CollectionComponent, SerialComponent)
because journal articles routinely carry arXiv or similar identifiers.

class.description: Subject class or category used by the server (e.g. "cs.CL").

id.description: The identifier on the preprint server (e.g. "2301.00001").

server.description:
The preprint server name in canonical lowercase form.

Producers may supply mixed-case values such as "arXiv" or "SSRN";
implementations should treat the field case-insensitively and compare
or normalize on the lowercase form.

authority.description: Legislative body conducting the hearing (e.g., "U.S. Senate Committee on Finance")

author.description: Creator or maintainer of the bibliography dataset.

title.description: Human-readable title for the bibliography dataset.

oneOf.description: A monograph, such as a book or a report, is a monolithic work published or produced as a complete entity.

A component of a larger Monograph, such as a chapter in a book.
The parent monograph is referenced by its ID.

A component of a larger serial publication; for example a journal or newspaper article.
The parent serial is referenced by its ID.
A collection of works, such as an anthology or proceedings.
A legal case (court decision).
A statute or legislative act.
An international treaty or agreement.
A legislative or administrative hearing.
An administrative regulation.
A legal brief or filing.
A classic work with standard citation forms.
A patent.
A research dataset.
A technical standard or specification.
Software or source code.

page.description: First page of case in reporter

title.description: Case name (e.g., "Brown v. Board of Education")

Monograph.description: A monograph, such as a book or a report, is a monolithic work published or produced as a complete entity.

archive.description: Archive or repository name for unpublished material.

archive-info.description: Structured archival location metadata. When present, preferred over legacy `archive` and `archive_location`.

archive-location.description: Archive location, shelfmark, or call number for unpublished material.

container-title.description: Parent or container title for monographic interviews and similar sources.

genre.description:
Free-text genre descriptor using kebab-case canonical forms (e.g., `"phd-thesis"`, `"short-film"`).
See `docs/reference/GENRE_AND_MEDIUM_VALUES.md` for canonical values and `docs/policies/ENUM_VOCABULARY_POLICY.md`.

guest.description: Guest for interview or podcast-style references.

medium.description:
Free-text medium descriptor using kebab-case canonical forms (e.g., `"film"`, `"television"`).
See `docs/reference/GENRE_AND_MEDIUM_VALUES.md` for canonical values and `docs/policies/ENUM_VOCABULARY_POLICY.md`.

recipient.description: Recipient for personal communications such as letters or emails.

MonographComponentType.description: Discriminates monograph-component subtypes for style-directed formatting.

oneOf.description: A chapter within a book or edited volume.
A document component that does not fit a more specific subtype.

MonographType.description: Discriminates monograph subtypes for style-directed formatting.

oneOf.description: A book or monograph (default).
A technical manual or user guide.
A technical or institutional report.
An academic thesis or dissertation.
A webpage or standalone web document.
A standalone post (e.g., social media, forum).
An interview treated as a standalone monographic source.
An unpublished manuscript or archival document.

A preprint hosted on a preprint server (arXiv, bioRxiv, SSRN, etc.).

The preprint server has a custodial relationship with the work (hosting and
preservation), not an editorial one. This parallels an archived manuscript
held by a repository.
A letter, email, or other personal communication.
A generic standalone document that does not fit a more specific subtype.

MultilingualComplex.description:
Complex multilingual representation with original, transliterations, and translations.

Allows capturing the original text in its native script, along with transliterations
(phonetic representations in different scripts) and translations (semantic equivalents
in different languages). This is essential for accurately rendering bibliographies in
multilingual and non-Latin-script contexts.

lang.description: ISO 639/BCP 47 language code for the original text.

original.description: The text in its original script and language.

translations.description:
Translations of the text into other languages.

Keys are ISO 639/BCP 47 language codes (e.g., "en" for English, "fr" for French).
Values are the translated text.

transliterations.description:
Transliterations/Transcriptions of the original text into other scripts.

Keys are typically script codes (e.g., "Latn" for Latin) or full BCP 47 tags.
Values are the transliterated or transcribed text.

lang.description: ISO 639/BCP 47 language code for the original name.

original.description: The name in its original script.

MultilingualString.description:
A string that can be represented in multiple languages and scripts.

This is an enum that supports both simple strings and complex multilingual representations.
Use `Simple` for basic strings and `Complex` when you need to track original language,
transliterations, and translations.

anyOf.description: A simple string in a single language.
A complex multilingual string with original, transliterations, and translations.

NumOrStr.description:
A value that could be either a number or a string.

Used for fields that may contain numeric or string values, such as issue numbers,
volume numbers, or similar identifiers that can be formatted as either type.
The `Display` implementation shows the value in its appropriate form.

Parent.description: A parent reference (either embedded or by ID).

Parent2.description: A parent reference (either embedded or by ID).

authority.description: Patent office (e.g., "U.S. Patent and Trademark Office")

short-title.description: Optional short form of the parent title for style-directed rendering.

SerialComponent.description:
A component of a larger serial publication; for example a journal or newspaper article.
The parent serial is referenced by its ID.

parent.description: The parent work, such as a magazine or journal.

SerialComponentType.description: Discriminates serial-component subtypes for style-directed formatting.

oneOf.description: A peer-reviewed or editorial article in a journal, magazine, or newspaper.
A post within an online serial (blog, news site, social feed).
A review published in a serial (book review, film review, etc.).

SimpleName.description: A simple name is just a string, with an optional location.

license.description:
SPDX license identifier preferred (e.g., `"MIT"`, `"GPL-3.0-only"`, `"Apache-2.0"`).
See <https://spdx.org/licenses/> for the authoritative identifier list.

status.description:
Publication status. Canonical controlled-vocabulary values: `"published"`, `"draft"`, `"withdrawn"`.
See `docs/policies/ENUM_VOCABULARY_POLICY.md` for matching rules.

StructuredName.description: A structured name is a name broken down into its constituent parts.

StructuredTitle.description: Where title parts are meaningful, use this struct; Citum processors will not parse title strings.

Subtitle.description: The subtitle can either be a string, as is the common case, or a vector of strings.

Title.description: A title can be a single string, a structured title, or a multilingual title.

anyOf.description: A title in a single language.
A structured title in multiple languages.
